items.title: US senators ask for review of Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' safety data, citing Reuters report
items.topic: US senators ask for review of Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' safety data, citing Reuters report
items.summary: US senators have requested a review of Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' (FSD) safety data, citing a Reuters report. The senators are concerned about the accuracy and completeness of the data provided by Tesla to regulators. This scrutiny highlights ongoing safety concerns and regulatory oversight in the deployment of autonomous vehicle technologies.
items.abstract: US senators have requested a review of Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' (FSD) safety data, citing a Reuters report. The senators are concerned about the accuracy and completeness of the data provided by Tesla to regulators. This scrutiny highlights ongoing safety concerns and regulatory oversight in the deployment of autonomous vehicle technologies.

items.title: Tesla presented misleading ‘Full Self-Driving’ safety data to European regulators
items.topic: Tesla presented misleading ‘Full Self-Driving’ safety data to European regulators
items.summary: Tesla has been accused of presenting misleading safety data for its 'Full Self-Driving' (FSD) technology to European regulators, according to a Reuters report. The company is under investigation for potentially providing incomplete or inaccurate information, which could impact the regulatory approval and public trust in Tesla's autonomous driving capabilities.
items.abstract: Tesla has been accused of presenting misleading safety data for its 'Full Self-Driving' (FSD) technology to European regulators, according to a Reuters report. The company is under investigation for potentially providing incomplete or inaccurate information, which could impact the regulatory approval and public trust in Tesla's autonomous driving capabilities.

items.title: Fine-tuned multimodal large language model for autonomous state cognition system of shape-recognition 6-bar tensegrity integrated with flexible sensors
items.topic: Fine-tuned multimodal large language model for autonomous state cognition system of shape-recognition 6-bar tensegrity integrated with flexible sensors
items.summary: Researchers have developed a fine-tuned multimodal large language model (LLM) for an autonomous state cognition system that integrates shape-recognition in a 6-bar tensegrity structure with flexible sensors. This model enhances the perception and decision-making capabilities of autonomous systems, particularly in complex and dynamic environments, advancing the field of autonomous vehicle cognition.
items.abstract: Researchers have developed a fine-tuned multimodal large language model (LLM) for an autonomous state cognition system that integrates shape-recognition in a 6-bar tensegrity structure with flexible sensors. This model enhances the perception and decision-making capabilities of autonomous systems, particularly in complex and dynamic environments, advancing the field of autonomous vehicle cognition.

items.title: EXCLUSIVE: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving tech in Europe over speeding concerns
items.topic: EXCLUSIVE: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving tech in Europe over speeding concerns
items.summary: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving technology in Europe due to concerns over speeding. The country's regulators are evaluating the safety and compliance of Tesla's FSD system, which could lead to restrictions or bans on the technology in the European market. This development underscores the ongoing regulatory challenges for autonomous vehicle deployment.
items.abstract: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving technology in Europe due to concerns over speeding. This development underscores the ongoing regulatory challenges for autonomous vehicle deployment.

items.title: Tesla teases new FSD features — but it’s still not the self-driving promised
items.topic: Tesla teases new FSD features — but it’s still not the self-driving promised
items.summary: Tesla has teased new features for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, but the company acknowledges that the technology is still not fully autonomous. The updates include improvements in navigation and object detection, but Tesla emphasizes that the system still requires driver supervision. This highlights the incremental nature of autonomous driving technology development.
items.abstract: Tesla has teased new features for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, but the company acknowledges that the technology is still not fully autonomous. The updates include improvements in navigation and object detection, but Tesla emphasizes that the system still requires driver supervision. This highlights the incremental nature of autonomous driving technology development.

items.title: Tesla Owners Are Using Doll Heads To Fool Full Self-Driving
items.topic: Tesla Owners Are Using Doll Heads To Fool Full Self-Driving
items.summary: Tesla owners are using doll heads to fool the Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, demonstrating a potential vulnerability in the technology. This behavior raises concerns about the reliability and safety of autonomous driving systems and the need for robust testing and validation processes to prevent such exploits.
items.abstract: Tesla owners are using doll heads to fool the Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, demonstrating a potential vulnerability in the technology. items.title: Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
items.topic: Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
items.summary: Google DeepMind is investing in multi-agent AI safety research to address the challenges of deploying multiple autonomous agents in complex environments. The research focuses on ensuring that AI systems can operate safely and ethically, particularly in scenarios where multiple agents interact, which is crucial for the development of autonomous vehicle fleets.
items.abstract: Google DeepMind is investing in multi-agent AI safety research to address the challenges of deploying multiple autonomous agents in complex environments. The research focuses on ensuring that AI systems can operate safely and ethically, particularly in scenarios where multiple agents interact, which is crucial for the development of autonomous vehicle fleets.

items.title: RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation
items.topic: RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation
items.summary: The arXiv paper 'RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation' proposes a lightweight, distilled VLA model called RT-VLA, which transfers the driving and reasoning capabilities of the state-of-the-art SimLingo model into a compact student through multi-level supervised distillation. RT-VLA preserves language-based reasoning and supports post-hoc explanation through offline language analysis of safety-critical driving moments without adding latency to real-time control. This approach enables the deployment of VLA models in real-time autonomous driving applications. The RT-VLA model is evaluated on a driving dataset and demonstrates improved performance and efficiency.
items.abstract: The arXiv paper 'RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation' proposes a lightweight, distilled VLA model called RT-VLA, which transfers the driving and reasoning capabilities of the state-of-the-art SimLingo model into a compact student through multi-level supervised distillation. RT-VLA preserves language-based reasoning and supports post-hoc explanation through offline language analysis of safety-critical driving moments without adding latency to real-time control. This approach enables the deployment of VLA models in real-time autonomous driving applications. The RT-VLA model is evaluated on a driving dataset and demonstrates improved performance and efficiency.

items.title: DriveJudge: Rethinking Autonomous Driving Evaluation with Vision-Language Models
items.topic: DriveJudge: Rethinking Autonomous Driving Evaluation with Vision-Language Models
items.summary: The arXiv paper 'DriveJudge: Rethinking Autonomous Driving Evaluation with Vision-Language Models' introduces DriveJudge, a driving evaluation agent that combines rule-grounded evaluation with Vision-Language Model (VLM) reasoning and selectively invokes physically-grounded deterministic rule functions after interpreting the environmental context. DriveJudge is trained and evaluated on a curated dataset and demonstrates improved driving evaluation capabilities. The approach enables more accurate and context-aware evaluation of autonomous driving policies. The DriveJudge framework has the potential to improve the safety and reliability of autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: The arXiv paper 'DriveJudge: Rethinking Autonomous Driving Evaluation with Vision-Language Models' introduces DriveJudge, a driving evaluation agent that combines rule-grounded evaluation with Vision-Language Model (VLM) reasoning and selectively invokes physically-grounded deterministic rule functions after interpreting the environmental context. DriveJudge is trained and evaluated on a curated dataset and demonstrates improved driving evaluation capabilities. The approach enables more accurate and context-aware evaluation of autonomous driving policies. The DriveJudge framework has the potential to improve the safety and reliability of autonomous vehicles.

items.title: ParkingTransformer: LLM-Enhanced End-to-End Trajectory Planning for Autonomous Parking
items.topic: ParkingTransformer: LLM-Enhanced End-to-End Trajectory Planning for Autonomous Parking
items.summary: The arXiv paper 'ParkingTransformer: LLM-Enhanced End-to-End Trajectory Planning for Autonomous Parking' proposes a novel framework called ParkingTransformer, which leverages multi-view perception and the scene understanding capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to plan trajectories for autonomous parking. ParkingTransformer combines trajectory queries with LLMs' implicit state features to interact directly with historical information and raw sensor data, eliminating the need for dense Bird's-View (BEV) representations. The approach enables more efficient and accurate trajectory planning for autonomous parking. The ParkingTransformer framework has the potential to improve the safety and convenience of autonomous vehicles.

ParkingTransformer combines trajectory queries with LLMs' implicit state features to interact directly with historical information and raw sensor data, eliminating the need for dense Bird's-View (BEV) representations. The approach enables more efficient and accurate trajectory planning for autonomous parking. The ParkingTransformer framework has the potential to improve the safety and convenience of autonomous vehicles.
